creative_brief: Hero image: split-screen showing a farmer's weathered hands holding freshly harvested produce on one side, and a family collecting a full grocery bag at the Brookvale hub on the other. Warm, natural light. Earthy tones with FarmThru green accents. Price comparison graphic in the hero section: two simple bar charts showing farmer share per kilo. Clean, editorial feel throughout. No stock photography.
headline: $3.80 per kilo goes to the farmer. $0.90 does at the supermarket.
subhead: A grocery store where most of your money actually reaches the people who grow your food.
hero_copy:
You spend hundreds on groceries every month. You assume the farmer gets a fair cut. They don't.

Supermarket supply chains pass your dollar through warehouses, wholesalers, and distribution centres. By the time it reaches the farm, there's almost nothing left.

FarmThru cuts all of that out. Farm to hub to you. Zero warehouses. Zero wholesalers. Your money goes where it should: to the people feeding you.

Now you can own a piece of the grocery store that pays farmers first.

sections.heading: You're already paying. The question is who gets it.
sections.body:
Every week, Australian families hand over grocery money trusting that someone, somewhere, is being paid fairly.

But the standard supply chain travels roughly 2,400km before food hits a shelf. Each stop takes a cut. The farmer absorbs the loss.

This isn't a broken system. It's working exactly as designed. It's just not designed for farmers. Or for you.

FarmThru exists because we believe your grocery dollar should do two things: feed your family well and pay the people who grew that food properly.

sections.heading: How it works: three steps, no middlemen
sections.body:
1. Browse and order online from 15 to 20 partner farms and suppliers across NSW and beyond.

2. We coordinate directly with farms like Bundarra Farm, Collins Wild Caught on the South Coast, Paris Creek Farms in South Australia, and Farmer Brown for eggs.

3. Collect from our Brookvale hub, Monday to Friday. Or choose delivery across Sydney, Central Coast, and Wollongong.

No subscription. No lock-in. No membership fees. Order when you want. Buy in bulk and save more.

Every product traces back to a named farm. We'll show you where your food comes from.

sections.heading: Real farms. Real names. That's the whole point.
sections.body:
Pork belly from Bundarra Farm. Wild-caught Alaskan salmon from Collins Wild Caught on the NSW South Coast. Carbon-neutral biodynamic dairy from Paris Creek Farms in Meadows, SA. Eggs from Farmer Brown.

Rachel Ward, actor turned regenerative farming advocate, runs Rachel's Farm in Kempsey, NSW. She's a key partner and supplier to FarmThru.

When Rachel talks about regenerative farming, she's standing in the paddock where it happens. That's the standard we hold every supplier to.

We only stock food we'd feed our own families. If we can't name the farm, it doesn't go on the shelf.

sections.heading: Your groceries can fund a different kind of food system
sections.body:
Most people want to support farmers. But the checkout aisle doesn't give you that option.

FarmThru does. And now, through our equity crowdfunding campaign, you can go further. You can own a piece of a grocery store that pays farmers first.

This isn't charity. It's a chance to put your values and your money pointing in the same direction.

Join the waitlist to be first in when the campaign opens. Whether you want to shop, invest, or both, you'll get early access.

Your $5 refundable deposit secures your spot on the VIP list.

sections.heading: What people ask us
sections.body:
"Do I have to commit to a subscription?"
No. FarmThru is not a subscription box. It's an online grocery store. Order when you want. No lock-in.

"Where do I collect?"
Our Brookvale hub: Unit 23, 10-18 Orchard Rd, Brookvale NSW 2100. Monday to Friday. Delivery also available to Sydney, Central Coast, and Wollongong.

"Is equity crowdfunding risky?"
Yes. All investment carries risk, including the potential loss of your entire investment. We believe in transparency, which means acknowledging that honestly. Read the offer document carefully before deciding.

"What does the $5 VIP deposit do?"
It's fully refundable. It puts you at the front of the line when the campaign launches. No obligation to invest.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru's equity crowdfunding campaign is conducted under the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) crowd-sourced funding framework. Investing in early-stage companies carries significant risk, including the potential loss of your entire investment. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. No returns are projected or guaranteed. You should read the offer document in full and consider seeking independent financial advice before making any investment decision. The $5 VIP deposit is fully refundable and does not constitute an investment or obligation to invest.
